name: Samarkanda
description: Quadruple resampling performance delay. Four channels (A·B·C·D) of 0.5ms–15s each, with analog (tape/BBD-like pitch-shifting) and digital (granular crossfade) behaviours, freeze, reverse, 1V/oct loop playback, linkable groups, and lossless digital stacking.

controls.name: TIME
controls.description: Large rotary delay-time dial. Exponential scale tied to the RANGE switch. Also doubles as loop-length control while HOLD is engaged.
controls.detail: X1: 0.5–150ms · X10: 5–1500ms · X100: 50–15000ms · in sync/tap shows discrete factors 1:8…8:1

controls.name: RANGE
controls.description: Three-position TIME scale selector. Ignored when the channel is externally synced (factor scale replaces it).
controls.detail: X1 · X10 · X100 · overlaps at boundaries

controls.name: BEHAVIOUR
controls.description: Switches the delay algorithm between tape/BBD-like resampling and granular crossfade. Can change at any time.
controls.detail: DIGITAL = crossfade between read positions, no pitch change · ANALOG = variable sample rate with tape/BBD pitch shift

controls.name: FBCK
controls.description: Feedback amount. Full-turn ≈ 10V of CV equivalent. Nonlinear response — sharp from 0% toward 80%, slow over the last few % toward full.
controls.detail: 0–10 · beyond 50% invokes ANALOG soft-clip or DIGITAL compressor · 10 = infinite (use HOLD for true sound-on-sound)

controls.name: MIX
controls.description: Dry/wet balance at MIX OUT. Bipolar CV offset via MIX jack.

controls.name: REVERSE
controls.description: Lit button toggling reverse-delay playback. Also a gate/trigger input on the associated jack — rising edge flips state.
controls.detail: rising edge flips state · on reverses buffer playback

controls.name: HOLD
controls.description: Lit button freezing the buffer into a loop. Also a gate/trigger input. Loop length = current TIME; TIME/RANGE/CV then act on loop length.
controls.detail: freezes buffer · TIME becomes loop-length · with ANALOG + 1V/oct = tuned loop playback

controls.name: TAP TEMPO
controls.description: Lit button — taps set the time base. Also indicates sync state via colour.
controls.detail: green = continuous · yellow = tap · red = external sync

controls.name: LINK
controls.description: Button linking a channel to its left neighbour as follower. Leftmost linked channel = master. Can extend groups (A+B+C+D) or pair into A+B · C+D etc.
controls.detail: linked TIME dial becomes ±relative around 1:1 at noon · ranges X1/X10/X100 rescale relative span

controls.name: PURGE / SHIFT
controls.description: Multi-function button. Momentary hold + release = clear every buffer. Also the SHIFT modifier for secondary functions.
controls.detail: hold+release = purge · SHIFT + REVERSE = invert feedback polarity · SHIFT + HOLD = invert TIME CV polarity

controls.name: IN / MIX LEDs
controls.description: Dual multicolour peak indicators for analog input and mix-out levels, with PPM-style discharge.
controls.detail: green safe · yellow hot · red soft-clip engaged · LED off = no analog cable (digital normalised)

inputs.name: INPUT
inputs.description: Audio input for the channel. With no cable, digital MIX data from the previous channel is virtually passed in lossless — enabling clean stacking.

inputs.name: FB INPUT
inputs.description: Return for the external feedback loop. Digitally normalised to the WET OUT of the same channel — patch breaks normalisation to insert external processing.

inputs.name: TIME
inputs.description: CV over delay time. 1V ≈ ×2 in the continuous ranges (effectively 1V/oct in loop-playback mode).

inputs.name: SYNC
inputs.description: External clock input. Patching activates sync mode (TAP TEMPO red); RANGE switch is ignored and factor scale replaces it.

inputs.name: REVERSE
inputs.description: Gate/trigger input. Rising edge flips the REVERSE button state.

inputs.name: HOLD
inputs.description: Gate/trigger input. Rising edge flips the HOLD button state.

inputs.name: FBCK
inputs.description: Bipolar CV added to the feedback knob. Full 10V step equals a full-knob turn.

inputs.name: MIX
inputs.description: Bipolar CV offsetting the dry/wet knob.

outputs.name: MIX OUT
outputs.description: Dry + wet blend per MIX controls. Also the source of digital stacking into the next channel's INPUT when that channel has no cable patched.

outputs.name: WET OUT
outputs.description: Direct access to the delayed signal only. Source of the normalised internal feedback loop (broken by patching FB INPUT).

behaviors.name: Analog vs digital time-change behaviour
behaviors.description: ANALOG models a fixed-rate buffer read at a variable sample rate — every change of TIME squeezes/stretches what is in the buffer, pitching it tape/BBD-style. DIGITAL crossfades between buffer read positions at a fixed rate — delay time can jump without pitch change. Switchable live; HOLD additionally changes the switch into a loop-playback style selector.

behaviors.name: Lossless digital channel stacking
behaviors.trigger: no cable in INPUT of channel B/C/D
behaviors.description: When INPUT is unpatched, the MIX OUT of the previous channel is passed digitally (skipping D/A + A/D) into the next channel. Four channels default to a stacked cascade of delays with intricate compounding echoes, and the feedback paths follow the same smart normalisation.

behaviors.name: Linked channels relative to a master
behaviors.description: Linked channels follow the master's TIME/MIX/FBCK controls relatively — knobs act as bipolar offsets around the master value. TIME range switch on linked channel scales the offset span (X100 = broad, X1 = very fine). Eight link permutations possible; two independent pairs (A+B and C+D) is a common stereo layout.

behaviors.name: Continuous / tap-tempo / external sync per channel
behaviors.description: Each channel independently operates in free (green), tap-tempo (yellow), or external-sync (red) mode. Patching a cable to SYNC forces sync; unplug returns to free. In synced modes, TIME knob jumps between factor 1:8 to 8:1 and RANGE is ignored.

behaviors.name: Reverse delay (buffer played backwards)
behaviors.description: Segments of the buffer are played backwards — periodic discontinuity when the read pointer meets the oldest sample, masked by short fades. Adding feedback re-reverses the sound on the second repetition; to keep directional reverse with feedback, stack two channels: first reverse/dry, second forward with feedback.

behaviors.name: HOLD freezes and loops the buffer
behaviors.description: Captures the current buffer as a loop. In DIGITAL, TIME sweeps loop length without pitch. In ANALOG, TIME resamples the frozen loop — so TIME CV + 1V/oct lets you play the loop chromatically from a sequencer (Samarkanda as a crude tuned sampler).

behaviors.name: 1V/oct tuned loop playback
behaviors.trigger: HOLD ON · BEHAVIOUR = ANALOG · SHIFT+HOLD combo to set CV polarity if needed
behaviors.description: Manual PURGE+HOLD sets TIME CV to +1V/oct sensitivity. Freeze a single-cycle or short phrase into the buffer, then play it via a keyboard/sequencer → TIME. Different initial TIME knob settings yield different loop lengths = different 'patches' to play.

behaviors.name: Feedback polarity inversion
behaviors.description: Flips feedback polarity from positive to negative for that channel. Subtle on long delays but dramatic on very short delays — shifts the notch/peak positions of the resonator-like comb pattern. Per-channel and persistent across power cycles.

behaviors.name: TIME CV polarity inversion
behaviors.description: Inverts the direction of TIME CV for that channel (factory +1V/oct → -1V/oct). Useful for playing tuned loops from sequencers that output negative voltages, or inverting sweep direction. Big TIME knob direction is unchanged.

behaviors.name: External feedback loops per channel
behaviors.description: Breaks the digital normalisation from WET OUT back to FBCK and lets you insert any external processing into the repeat path — filters, distortions, reverbs, even another Samarkanda channel for feedback-delay-network (FDN) reverb topologies.

behaviors.name: Soft-clip (ANALOG) vs compressor (DIGITAL) on feedback overdrive
behaviors.description: ANALOG applies a modeled soft-clip to emulate classic analog saturation at the repeat stage. DIGITAL applies a transparent compressor to tame amplitude buildup without audible colour. Gain structure behaviour thus differs sharply between the two modes.

behaviors.name: PURGE clears all buffers
behaviors.trigger: hold PURGE then release without pressing other buttons
behaviors.description: Instantly wipes all four buffers. Handy after runaway feedback or when changing sources — no need to power-cycle. Holding PURGE alone while pressing REVERSE/HOLD invokes the secondary (polarity) functions instead.

patchIdeas.name: Two independent stereo delays (A+B, C+D)
patchIdeas.patch: LINK A↔B and C↔D · set knobs in B and D to noon (1:1) · channels B/D follow A/C. Delay-time, feedback, mix changes in A/C affect both pairs · patch stereo L/R into A and C
patchIdeas.diagram:
Samarkanda:
* LINK A-B: ON
* LINK C-D: ON
* BEHAVIOUR A/B: ANALOG
* BEHAVIOUR C/D: ANALOG

- SourceL (Out) -> Samarkanda (INPUT A)
- SourceR (Out) -> Samarkanda (INPUT C)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT B) -> Output (L)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT D) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Stacked granular cascade with compounding echoes
patchIdeas.patch: Default stacked config · all four channels DIGITAL · each TIME at a different subdivision of clock · MIX at 100% per channel · cascading taps produce dense, overlapping granular texture
patchIdeas.diagram:
Samarkanda:
* BEHAVIOUR A/B/C/D: DIGITAL
* TIME A: 1/4
* TIME B: 1/8
* TIME C: 3/8
* TIME D: 1/2

- ClockSrc (Out) c> Samarkanda (SYNC A)
- ClockSrc (Out) c> Samarkanda (SYNC B)
- ClockSrc (Out) c> Samarkanda (SYNC C)
- ClockSrc (Out) c> Samarkanda (SYNC D)
- Voice (Out) -> Samarkanda (INPUT A)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT D) -> Output (L)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT D) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Tuned loop sampler (1V/oct loop playback)
patchIdeas.patch: Record a phrase into A · press HOLD · BEHAVIOUR ANALOG · PURGE+HOLD combo to ensure +1V/oct polarity · keyboard → TIME A · TIME knob sets base loop length
patchIdeas.diagram:
Samarkanda:
* A BEHAVIOUR: ANALOG
* A HOLD: ON
* A RANGE: X10

- Voice (Out) -> Samarkanda (INPUT A)
- Kbd (Pitch) p> Samarkanda (TIME A)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT A) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Ping-pong with cross-coupled feedback
patchIdeas.patch: Patch WET OUT A → FB INPUT B and WET OUT B → FB INPUT A · link off · different TIMEs per channel · stereo bounces between sides
patchIdeas.diagram:
Samarkanda:
* LINK: OFF
* BEHAVIOUR A/B: ANALOG

- Voice (Out) -> Samarkanda (INPUT A)
- Samarkanda (WET OUT A) -> Samarkanda (FB INPUT B)
- Samarkanda (WET OUT B) -> Samarkanda (FB INPUT A)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT A) -> Output (L)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT B) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Reverse verb via two stacked channels
patchIdeas.patch: Channel A: REVERSE ON, no feedback, full wet · Channel B: forward with long feedback, picks up A's reversed tail · gives true reversed-with-decay effect
patchIdeas.diagram:
Samarkanda:
* A REVERSE: ON
* A FBCK: 0
* A MIX: 10 (wet)
* B REVERSE: OFF
* B FBCK: 7

- Voice (Out) -> Samarkanda (INPUT A)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT B) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Tuned resonator
patchIdeas.patch: BEHAVIOUR ANALOG · RANGE X1 · FBCK near max · keyboard into TIME at 1V/oct · PURGE+HOLD set if needed · plucky resonant tones track pitch
patchIdeas.diagram:
Samarkanda:
* A BEHAVIOUR: ANALOG
* A RANGE: X1
* A FBCK: 9
* A MIX: 10

- Kbd (Pitch) p> Samarkanda (TIME A)
- Trig (Trig) t> VCA (CV)
- Noise (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Samarkanda (INPUT A)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT A) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Real-time granulator on one channel
patchIdeas.patch: BEHAVIOUR DIGITAL · RANGE X10 · FBCK 0 · MIX fully wet · random CV source into TIME CV · continuous grain scatter · increase FBCK for denser cloud
patchIdeas.diagram:
Samarkanda:
* A BEHAVIOUR: DIGITAL
* A RANGE: X10
* A FBCK: 3
* A MIX: 10

- Pad (Out) -> Samarkanda (INPUT A)
- Noise (SlewRnd) >> Samarkanda (TIME A)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT A) -> Reverb (In)
- Reverb (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Buffer freeze pad from live source
patchIdeas.patch: Play material into A · gate into HOLD jack captures on the fly · slow LFO into TIME CV creates drifting sustained pad · add ANALOG for tape drift, DIGITAL for stable pitch
patchIdeas.diagram:
Samarkanda:
* A BEHAVIOUR: DIGITAL
* A RANGE: X100

- Voice (Out) -> Samarkanda (INPUT A)
- Gate (Out) g> Samarkanda (HOLD A)
- Slow LFO (Out) >> Samarkanda (TIME A)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT A) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Maximum 60-second delay line
patchIdeas.patch: Stacked default · all RANGE X100 · all TIME max · MIX 100% · FBCK 0 in B/C/D · feedback only from D WET OUT back to A FB INPUT · effective ~60s delay
patchIdeas.diagram:
Samarkanda:
* A/B/C/D RANGE: X100
* A/B/C/D TIME: max
* A FBCK: 0
* B/C FBCK: 0
* D FBCK: 0

- Voice (Out) -> Samarkanda (INPUT A)
- Samarkanda (WET OUT D) -> Samarkanda (FB INPUT A)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT D) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: FDN-style reverb across four channels
patchIdeas.patch: All four stacked with short differing TIMEs · external matrix mixer cross-feeds WET OUTs into each channel's FB INPUT with different gains · shimmering dense early reflections
patchIdeas.diagram:
Samarkanda:
* A TIME: 37ms
* B TIME: 53ms
* C TIME: 71ms
* D TIME: 97ms
* A/B/C/D BEHAVIOUR: DIGITAL

- Voice (Out) -> Samarkanda (INPUT A)
- Samarkanda (WET OUT A) -> Matrix (In1)
- Samarkanda (WET OUT B) -> Matrix (In2)
- Samarkanda (WET OUT C) -> Matrix (In3)
- Samarkanda (WET OUT D) -> Matrix (In4)
- Matrix (Out1) -> Samarkanda (FB INPUT A)
- Matrix (Out2) -> Samarkanda (FB INPUT B)
- Matrix (Out3) -> Samarkanda (FB INPUT C)
- Matrix (Out4) -> Samarkanda (FB INPUT D)
- Samarkanda (MIX OUT D) -> Output (In)
